scenario_results.reason: The lane resolved a standard design-package status request only after the register, transmittal, and issue-history records aligned on the current A&E package.
Missing project, phase, discipline, and transmittal prerequisites produced a blocked-state response instead of speculative file delivery.
An unsupported request to treat an unsigned interim package as a final permit set was refused and escalated to the documented release owners.
A client-deliverable conversation that turned into design and code interpretation was handed to architect or engineer of record ownership without overreach.
Repeat issuance failures and recalled revisions triggered QA or QC and document-control escalation instead of another generic resend.
The lane rejected uncontrolled screenshots and email attachments as authoritative deliverables because the transmittal and CDE traceability path was missing.
Turnover handling preserved record drawings, COBie, systems-manual, O&M, and commissioning terminology rather than flattening the request into generic support.
Missing jurisdictional and responsible-professional release facts forced low-confidence escalation instead of unsupported seal or permit conclusions.
The package proved the industry overlay is material by requiring transmittal, issue-purpose, revision, and CDE evidence before treating a file set as current.
Conflicting public-owner and tenant release rules were surfaced as a policy-precedence issue requiring escalation.
Commercial scope and fee requests were rejected cleanly and routed to project-management or contracts owners.
Tenant-specific stage codes, classified EDMS controls, and secure-transfer rules were applied as stricter overlays without weakening the public baseline.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the service-support-and-resolution and cross-industry contract.
The package stays inside client deliverable handling and does not claim architect-of-record, engineer-of-record, permit, seal, legal, or commercial approval authority.
